description_long: This is a rustic water well constructed primarily from weathered wood and stone. The well features a circular stone base with a wooden bucket suspended by a rope, which is wound around a horizontal wooden spindle supported by two thick, natural tree trunks. The well is topped with a pitched, shingled wooden roof that appears aged and mossy, with some green vines climbing up the supports and roof. The spindle has a wooden handle for manual operation, and the bucket hangs directly over the open well. The overall design is robust and functional, with a natural, slightly overgrown appearance that suggests it is situated in a park or garden setting.
description: A rustic stone and wood water well with a mossy shingled roof, wooden bucket, and vine-covered supports. The well has a circular stone base and a hand-crank mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shingled roof, bucket suspended over the well, and the wooden crank handle on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular roof profile, thick tree trunk support, and partial view of the stone well opening.
description_view_2: Rear view with the mossy roof, bucket, and crank handle visible, plus more vines on the right support.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled roof, dense vine growth, and the Y-shaped wooden support structure.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden shingled roof supported by tree trunks; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden spindle with rope and bucket suspended over the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall forms the well's protective barrier, approximately waist-high.
attribute_decorative_elements: Vines and moss growing on the roof and supports.
